ddls:
CREATE TABLE "operators" (
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operator */,
  "operator_symbol" TEXT /* Symbol representing the operator (e.g., =, +=) */,
  "operator_type" TEXT /* Type of operator (e.g., assignment, arithmetic) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of operator functionality */,
  "syntax_example" TEXT /* Example of the operator in syntax */,
  "use_cases" TEXT /* Example scenarios where the operator is commonly used */,
  "related_operators" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of related operators */,
  "difficulty_level" TEXT /* Level of difficulty (e.g., easy, medium, hard) */,
  "references" TEXT /* Links to external resources for further reading */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each programming language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the programming language (e.g., Python, Java) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the language being referenced */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the programming language and its usage */,
  "official_website" TEXT /* Official website of the programming language */,
  "documentation_link" TEXT /* Link to the official documentation */,
  "community_size" INTEGER /* Estimated size of the community using the language */,
  "popularity_score" INTEGER /* Score indicating the popularity of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operator_language" (
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator */,
  "language_id" INTEGER /* ID of the programming language */,
  "usage_example" TEXT /* Example of how the operator is used in this language */,
  "is_core_operator" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the operator is a core part of the language */,
  "alternative_symbols" TEXT /* Alternative symbols used in the language */,
  "performance_impact" TEXT /* Description of the performance impact of the operator */,
  "examples_count" INTEGER /* Number of examples available for the operator in this language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operator_id", "language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user for notifications */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* User's first name */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* User's last name */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile picture */,
  "bio" TEXT /* User's bio or short description */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user is an administrator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "progress" (
  "progress_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each progress record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the learning module */,
  "completion_status" TEXT /* Status of completion (e.g., completed, in-progress) */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date of completion */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the user started the module */,
  "last_activity_date" TEXT /* Date of the last activity in the module */,
  "time_spent" INTEGER /* Time spent by the user on the module in minutes */,
  "score" INTEGER /* User's score in the module */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("progress_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_progress_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_progress_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "modules" (
  "module_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each module */,
  "module_name" TEXT /* Name of the learning module */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the module's content */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the module was created */,
  "difficulty_level" TEXT /* Level of difficulty (e.g., beginner, intermediate, advanced) */,
  "estimated_time" TEXT /* Estimated time to complete the module */,
  "prerequisites" TEXT /* Prerequisites for the module */,
  "is_featured" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the module is featured */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "exercises" (
  "exercise_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each exercise */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module the exercise belongs to */,
  "exercise_text" TEXT /* Text description of the exercise */,
  "solution_code" TEXT /* Code solution for the exercise */,
  "difficulty_level" TEXT /* Level of difficulty (e.g., easy, medium, hard) */,
  "hints" TEXT /* Hints to help users solve the exercise */,
  "test_cases" TEXT /* Test cases to validate the user's solution */,
  "is_mandatory" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the exercise is mandatory */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("exercise_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_exercises_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing feedback */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module being reviewed */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (1 to 5) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or suggestions */,
  "is_anonymous" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the feedback is anonymous */,
  "is_flagged" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the feedback is flagged for review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_feedback_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "community_forums" (
  "post_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each post */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the post */,
  "post_content" TEXT /* Content of the post */,
  "post_date" TEXT /* Date the post was created */,
  "is_pinned" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the post is pinned */,
  "view_count" INTEGER /* Number of views the post has received */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the post has received */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_community_forums_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the comment */,
  "comment_content" TEXT /* Content of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was made */,
  "is_pinned" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the comment is pinned */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the comment has received */,
  "is_flagged" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the comment is flagged for review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES community_forums ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics record */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module being analyzed */,
  "user_count" INTEGER /* Number of users engaging with the module */,
  "completion_rate" REAL /* Percentage of users that completed the module */,
  "date_recorded" TEXT /* Date when the analytics data was recorded */,
  "average_score" REAL /* Average score of users in the module */,
  "most_common_feedback" TEXT /* Most common feedback for the module */,
  "user_demographics" TEXT /* Demographic information of users engaging with the module */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the notification has been read */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., new module, reminder) */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module related to the notification (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
